page: Amazon Web Services m8a.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by AMD EPYC 9R45 processors. It features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local NVMe storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 34,342.68 GB/sec and uncached large block transfers at 1,037.85 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 10%. Computational capabilities are further highlighted by top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core, ray tracing, and software compilation tests. For database and memory-bound operations, the server achieves top-tier status in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests, though Passmark memory scores fall into the top 25% category.

The bare-metal architecture provides direct hardware access, eliminating hypervisor overhead and maximizing resource density for large-scale deployments. However, the absence of local storage requires external storage configuration, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the network setup. The server is highly efficient for CPU-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, static web serving, and software compilation. It also supports LLM inference, achieving top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, though text generation speeds for small models remain average.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, offering a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and GPU hardware. The server delivers top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-core CPU benchmarks, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Network connectivity is supported by a 75 Gbps baseline bandwidth. While the lack of local NVMe storage represents a key architectural tradeoff, the bare-metal design eliminates hypervisor overhead, making it highly efficient for massive parallel processing, software compilation, static web serving, and large-scale database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server featuring 192 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A bare-metal platform featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and DDR5 memory for high-throughput parallel computing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 cores operating at 4.5 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput connectivity
Suitable for large-scale databases, software compilation, and web serving
